assessment.title: JoraIQ Company-Based Assessment
assessment.description: Placement-readiness assessment covering very-hard DSA, hard SQL, OOPs and hard Core CS MCQs.

questions.title: Enterprise Incident SLA Optimization
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
An enterprise support team handles incidents where some incidents can start only after their prerequisite incidents are completed. Each incident has a resolution time and an SLA deadline. One engineer works on one incident at a time. The team wants to know the maximum number of incidents that can be completed within their SLA while still respecting all prerequisite dependencies.

Problem Statement
You are given n incidents numbered from 0 to n-1. dependencies[i] = [a, b] means incident a depends on incident b, so b must be completed before a. durations[i] is the time needed to resolve incident i. deadlines[i] is the latest completion time for incident i to be counted as on-time. You may resolve incidents in any valid order, and you may still resolve an incident even if it misses its SLA, but it will not increase the on-time count. Return the maximum number of incidents that can be completed on or before their deadlines. If the dependency graph has a cycle, return -1.

Input Example
n = 4
dependencies = [[2,0],[2,1],[3,2]]
durations = [2,2,3,1]
deadlines = [2,4,7,8]
Expected Output
4
expected_approach: Bitmask dynamic programming over resolved incident subsets
Track minimum completion time per subset

open_test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue
Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

questions.title: Same-Day Delivery Route Optimization
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A logistics company runs a same-day delivery system in a large city.

There are multiple delivery hubs and thousands of customer locations. Roads between locations have travel times. However, some roads are affected by traffic and can only be used after a certain time.

Each package has a pickup hub, a customer delivery location, and a latest delivery deadline.

A delivery partner starts from location 0 at time 0. The partner can carry only one package at a time.

For every package, the partner must travel from the current location to the package pickup hub, pick up the package instantly, travel from pickup hub to customer location, and deliver before or exactly at the deadline.

The partner may choose the order of packages. You need to find the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Problem Statement
You are given n locations numbered from 0 to n-1, m directed roads, and k packages.

Each road is [u, v, travelTime, openTime]. This means there is a directed road from u to v. The road can only be used at or after openTime. If the delivery partner reaches location u before openTime, they must wait until openTime. Arrival time at v is max(currentTime, openTime) + travelTime.

Each package is [pickupLocation, deliveryLocation, deadline].

The delivery partner starts at location 0 at time 0. Return the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Input Example
n = 6
roads = [[0,1,4,0],[1,2,3,0],[2,3,5,10],[0,4,2,0],[4,3,10,0],[3,5,2,0],[1,5,20,0],[4,2,6,5],[3,2,1,0]]
packages = [[1,3,18],[4,5,20],[2,5,25],[1,5,30]]

Expected Output
2

Note: roads are directed. The example includes road [3,2,1,0] so a route can continue from the first delivery area to another pickup hub. Without that directed connector, only one package is deliverable.

open_test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open
Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

questions.title: Global Backup Deduplication Range Analytics
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A backup platform receives files from many machines. Each file is represented by chunk hashes. Storage leaders now want to answer audit questions: within a selected range of files, how many distinct chunks would be stored if deduplication were applied only to that range? A chunk repeated inside the same file should be counted once for that file, and a chunk appearing in multiple files in the selected range should be counted once for the range.

Problem Statement
You are given files, where files[i] is the list of chunk hashes present in file i. You are also given queries, where each query [left, right] asks for the number of unique chunk hashes appearing in files left through right inclusive. Return one answer per query in the same order.

Input Example
files = [["a","b","a"],["b","c"],["a","d"],["e"]]
queries = [[0,1],[1,2],[0,3]]
Expected Output
[3,4,5]
constraints: Total number of chunks across all files <= 500000
expected_approach: Remove duplicate chunks within each file
Track last file index where each chunk appeared

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

questions.title: Branching Versioned Key-Value Store
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A design platform allows users to create alternative versions of the same project. A new version can be created from any previous version, not only from the latest version. Each new version changes exactly one design parameter while inheriting all other parameters from its base version. Users can later ask for the value of any parameter in any version.

Problem Statement
Design a branching versioned key-value store. Version 0 is empty. set(baseVersion, key, value) creates a new version from baseVersion, updates key to value in that new version, and returns the new version number. get(version, key) returns the value of key visible in that version. If the key does not exist in that version, return "NULL".

Operations Example
v1 = set(0, "height", 10)
v2 = set(v1, "width", 5)
v3 = set(v1, "height", 12)
get(v2, "height") -> 10
get(v3, "width") -> NULL
get(v3, "height") -> 12
constraints: Version numbers are created sequentially starting from 1
expected_approach: Represent versions as a persistent structure
Coordinate-compress keys or use persistent maps
Each set creates a new root from a base version
Each get queries the selected version root
O(log numberOfKeys) per operation with persistent segment tree, or expected logarithmic/persistent map approach

open_test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates
Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

questions.title: Transaction Fraud Ring Detection
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A payment platform wants to detect coordinated fraud rings, not just high transaction volume. An account A is considered to have a suspicious directed link to account B if A sends more than k transactions to B within any continuous window of t seconds. A fraud ring exists when suspicious links form a directed cycle among two or more accounts.

Problem Statement
You are given transactions sorted by timestamp. Each transaction is [fromAccount, toAccount, timestamp]. Build a directed edge from X to Y if X sent more than k transactions to Y within any t-second window, where end - start <= t. Return all account IDs that belong to at least one suspicious directed cycle. The output should be sorted lexicographically.

Input Example
transactions = [["A","B",1],["A","B",2],["A","B",3],["B","A",4],["B","A",5],["B","A",6],["C","D",7]]
k = 2
t = 3
Expected Output
["A","B"]
expected_approach: Group stream by ordered account pair
Sliding window using deque per pair
Build directed graph only for suspicious pairs
Run SCC algorithm such as Tarjan or Kosaraju
Return accounts in SCCs of size >= 2 or with self-loop if applicable

open_test_cases.purpose: One direction not suspicious due to window

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Cycle edges can appear far apart
Self transactions not enough in window

test_cases.purpose: One direction not suspicious due to window
Cycle edges can appear far apart
Self transactions not enough in window

questions.title: Delivery Promise Breach Analysis
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
An online retailer promises a delivery timestamp for every customer order. One order may be shipped in multiple packages. For customer experience reporting, an order should be treated as late only when the final delivered package arrives after the promised timestamp.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag High-Risk Customer Experience Cases. A customer should be flagged if: (1) they have at least 5 delivered, non-cancelled orders in the last 90 days, (2) at least 40% of those orders were delivered late, and (3) the average delay across late orders is at least 1 day. Use the final delivered package timestamp at order level. Return only flagged customers.

questions.title: Backup Failure Streak Risk Analysis
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
A backup platform runs scheduled backup jobs for many client servers. The operations team wants to identify active servers whose latest backup health is risky. A server is risky when its latest backup jobs show a continuing failure streak, not just because it had failures in the past.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return active servers whose latest backup status is FAILED and whose current consecutive failure streak is at least 3 jobs. Ignore inactive servers and inactive policies. For each risky server, return the streak start date, streak end date, consecutive failure count, latest status, and latest error code.
expected_approach: Use window functions for job ordering
Return only latest FAILED streaks with count >= 3

questions.title: License Utilization Review
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
A software subscription business sells seat-based licenses to customers. Some customers buy many seats but only a few assigned users actively use the product. Customer success wants to identify accounts that may need license optimization support before renewal.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag customers for License Utilization Review if: (1) they have an active paid contract with at least 10 purchased seats, (2) their average daily active users in the last 30 days is less than 50% of purchased seats, or (3) they had zero valid product usage in the last 30 days. Exclude trial contracts and internal/test users. Count usage only for users who had an assigned license on the usage date.
expected_approach: LEFT JOIN to include zero-usage customers
HAVING / CASE logic for review flag

questions.title: ServiceNow Incident Workflow Engine
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
ServiceNow handles different types of IT service tickets such as Incident, Service Request, Change Request and Problem Ticket. Each ticket type has a different workflow. The company wants to build a workflow engine where new ticket types can be added in the future without changing the existing workflow execution logic.

Problem Statement
The system should support creating different ticket types, moving each ticket to its next valid state, preventing invalid transitions, returning the current state of a ticket, and adding a new ticket type without changing existing core engine code.

domain_rules: Incident tickets follow New -> In Progress -> Resolved -> Closed.
Service Requests follow Draft -> Submitted -> Approved -> Fulfilled -> Closed.
Change Requests require Approval before Implementation and Review before Closed.
Problem Tickets support Investigation -> Root Cause Identified -> Workaround Provided -> Closed.
Invalid transitions must be rejected with a clear error or failed result.
required_components: A common Ticket abstraction or interface
Separate workflow/state behavior per ticket type
A WorkflowEngine or service that delegates transition rules
A way to query current ticket state
A response object or exception strategy for invalid transitions
design_constraints: Adding a new ticket type should not require editing existing engine transition code.
Avoid large if/else or switch blocks on ticket type inside the core engine.
Keep ticket data encapsulated; external code should not mutate state directly.
acceptance_signals: Uses State, Strategy, or equivalent polymorphic design.
Demonstrates at least two ticket workflows with different transitions.
Shows how a new workflow can be registered or introduced with minimal change.
Separates workflow rules from ticket identity and storage.
red_flags: Single procedural function with hard-coded ticket type checks
Public mutable state with no transition validation
Design cannot add a new workflow without modifying existing workflow engine logic

starter_code.python: # Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.javascript: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.java: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.cpp: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.

questions.title: Amazon Multi-Mode Payment Processing System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Amazon supports different payment modes such as Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, Wallet, Gift Card and Cash on Delivery. Each payment mode has different rules. The system should allow new modes like BNPL, EMI or International Card without changing the core checkout flow.

Problem Statement
The system should support processing payment using different payment modes, validating payment before processing, returning payment success or failure, applying payment-specific rules, and adding new payment modes without modifying checkout logic.

domain_rules: Credit Card and Debit Card require card validation and bank authorization.
UPI requires VPA validation and payment confirmation.
Wallet requires sufficient wallet balance before debit.
Gift Card applies available gift balance before remaining payment.
Cash on Delivery can be accepted only for eligible orders and serviceable locations.
required_components: A PaymentMethod interface or abstract base
Concrete payment implementations for at least three modes
CheckoutService depending on the abstraction rather than concrete classes
Validation and processing methods with success/failure result
A factory, registry, or dependency injection approach to choose payment mode
design_constraints: Adding BNPL, EMI, or International Card should not require modifying CheckoutService.
Do not place all payment rules inside one if/else chain.
Payment-specific validation should live with the payment strategy.
Checkout flow should orchestrate payment without knowing internal mode rules.
acceptance_signals: Demonstrates polymorphic process or pay method.
Returns consistent payment result objects or status values.
Handles validation failure separately from payment processing failure.
red_flags: CheckoutService switches on payment type for every rule
New payment mode requires editing existing checkout logic
Validation and processing responsibilities are mixed across unrelated classes

starter_code.python: # Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.javascript: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.java: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.cpp: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.

questions.title: Autodesk Design Export System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Autodesk tools allow users to export design files into PDF, DWG, DXF, STL, OBJ and PNG. Each format has different export behaviour. In the future, Autodesk may add SVG, IFC, STEP or GLTF. The system should allow adding new export formats without modifying the core design file class.

Problem Statement
The system should support exporting a design file into different formats, each format having its own logic, returning success/failure response, handling unsupported formats and adding new export formats without changing existing design file logic.

domain_rules: PDF export should support document metadata and page setup.
DWG and DXF exports should preserve CAD layers and units.
STL and OBJ exports should handle 3D mesh data.
PNG export should support raster resolution settings.
Unsupported formats must return a controlled failure instead of crashing.
required_components: A DesignFile domain object that does not implement every format directly
An Exporter interface or abstract base
Concrete exporters for at least three formats
An ExportService, registry, or factory for selecting exporters
A standard export result with status, format, and message or output path
design_constraints: Adding SVG, IFC, STEP, or GLTF should require adding a new exporter, not modifying DesignFile.
Avoid format-specific if/else chains in the DesignFile class.
Keep export settings explicit rather than hidden global state.
Separate validation of unsupported formats from export execution.
acceptance_signals: Uses composition so DesignFile delegates export behavior.
Shows concrete exporter classes with different behavior.
Provides a clear extension point for future formats.
Handles success and unsupported-format failure consistently.
red_flags: DesignFile has one method with switch/if branches for all formats
Adding a new format requires changing existing DesignFile logic

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A checkout service supports card, wallet, store credit and future payment modes. Each mode has different validation, retry and refund behavior. Checkout must not change when a new mode is added. Which design is most appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: A PaymentStrategy interface with separate implementations and a factory/registry to select the strategy

options.label: B
options.text: One checkout method with if-else for every payment mode

options.label: C
options.text: One Payment class with nullable fields for all payment types

options.label: D
options.text: Store the payment mode as a string and switch on it in every workflow

questions.explanation: Strategy with a registry/factory keeps checkout closed for modification and lets each payment method own its behavior.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A ticket can move through Draft, Submitted, Approved, Rejected and Closed. Invalid transitions must be blocked and each state has different allowed actions. Which approach best prevents transition bugs as states grow?

options.label: A
options.text: Keep all transition rules in UI buttons

options.label: B
options.text: Use State pattern where each state object controls valid next states and actions

options.label: C
options.text: Use integer status codes and update them directly from all services

options.label: D
options.text: Let the database accept any status and fix invalid rows later

questions.explanation: State pattern localizes transition rules and avoids scattered conditionals.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A reporting tool exports PDF today. Next quarter it may add Excel, JSON and XML. Existing export orchestration should not be edited for each new format. Which design best follows Open/Closed Principle?

options.label: A
options.text: ExportFormat interface with one class per format and dependency injection into orchestration

options.label: B
options.text: Add one more else-if every time a new format is added

options.label: C
options.text: Use a global variable called currentFormat

options.label: D
options.text: Put all export code in a single static utility method

questions.explanation: Adding a new implementation extends behavior without changing orchestration.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A single CheckoutManager validates coupons, calculates tax, talks to payment gateway, updates inventory, sends email and writes audit logs. Which principle is most clearly violated?
questions.explanation: The class has too many reasons to change, so SRP is violated.

questions.title: Networks
questions.prompt: Users can access a website by IP address but not by domain name. The server is reachable and HTTP is healthy. Which layer/component should be checked first?

options.label: A
options.text: DNS resolution path including resolver, authoritative records and cache

options.label: B
options.text: TCP congestion window on the web server

questions.explanation: IP works but domain fails, so DNS is the first suspect.

questions.title: Networks
questions.prompt: A real-time telemetry system sends frequent sensor updates. Losing one update is acceptable, but waiting for retransmission causes poor decisions. Which protocol is usually more suitable?

options.label: A
options.text: UDP, because it avoids connection overhead and retransmission delay

options.label: B
options.text: TCP, because every packet must always be retransmitted

questions.explanation: UDP is often used when freshness matters more than guaranteed delivery.

questions.title: Networks
questions.prompt: A load balancer keeps sending traffic to an instance whose process is alive but database connectivity from that instance is broken. What is the best improvement?

options.label: A
options.text: Use application-level health checks that verify critical downstream dependencies

options.label: B
options.text: Check only whether the VM responds to ping

options.label: D
options.text: Send all users permanently to one server

questions.explanation: A shallow ping can miss application-level failures.

questions.title: OS
questions.prompt: Four services acquire locks on Account, Wallet and Ledger in different orders. Rare production freezes occur. Which change most directly reduces deadlock risk?

options.label: A
options.text: Define and enforce a global lock acquisition order

questions.explanation: Global lock ordering removes circular wait, one of the deadlock conditions.

questions.title: OS
questions.prompt: A process has enough CPU but becomes very slow when its working set exceeds RAM. Disk I/O spikes and page faults increase sharply. What is the likely issue?
questions.explanation: High page faults and disk I/O under memory pressure indicate thrashing.

questions.title: OS
questions.prompt: Two threads increment a shared counter without synchronization. The final value is sometimes lower than expected. What is the root cause?

options.label: A
options.text: Race condition due to non-atomic read-modify-write

questions.explanation: Increment involves read, modify and write, which can interleave incorrectly.

questions.title: Cloud
questions.prompt: A service has low average CPU but high request latency because requests wait on database calls. Which autoscaling signal is likely better than CPU alone?

options.label: A
options.text: Queue length or p95 latency combined with request rate

options.label: B
options.text: Number of files in the code repository

questions.explanation: Latency/queue metrics capture user-facing bottlenecks better than CPU alone.

questions.title: Cloud
questions.prompt: A critical app runs on one VM in one availability zone. The VM is backed up nightly. What is still the biggest availability weakness?

options.label: A
options.text: A zone or VM failure can still cause downtime because there is no redundant active capacity

options.label: C
options.text: The app has too many logs

questions.explanation: Backups help recovery but do not provide immediate high availability.

questions.title: Cloud
questions.prompt: A data platform must store millions of images and logs cheaply, access them by key, and does not need POSIX file semantics. Which storage type is best?
questions.explanation: Object storage is designed for large-scale key-based unstructured data.

questions.title: Security
questions.prompt: Employees receive emails that look like vendor invoices and ask them to login through a fake page. Which combination is strongest?

options.label: A
options.text: Security awareness, email filtering, domain authentication and MFA

options.label: C
options.text: Allowing all attachments because vendors are trusted

questions.explanation: Phishing defense requires layered controls, not one weak measure.

questions.title: Security
questions.prompt: An app builds SQL using string concatenation with user input. Attackers can alter the WHERE clause. What is the best primary fix?
questions.explanation: Prepared statements separate code from data and prevent input from changing SQL structure.

questions.title: Security
questions.prompt: An analytics dashboard only needs read access to monthly sales tables, but its service account can drop any table in production. What is the issue?
questions.explanation: The account has more permission than required for its job.

questions.title: Architecture
questions.prompt: A system caches product prices for 24 hours. Prices update hourly, and customers sometimes see stale prices. Which tradeoff is being exposed?

options.label: D
options.text: Thread creation versus process creation only

questions.explanation: Long TTL improves performance but can increase stale reads.

questions.title: Architecture
questions.prompt: In an instruction pipeline, one instruction depends on the result of the immediately previous instruction. Without forwarding or stalls, the next instruction may read the old value. What is this called?
questions.explanation: A data hazard occurs when an instruction needs data not yet available.

questions.title: Excel
questions.prompt: A VLOOKUP formula starts returning wrong values after a new column is inserted in the lookup table. What is the best robust fix?

options.label: A
options.text: Use XLOOKUP or INDEX-MATCH with explicit return column references

options.label: B
options.text: Manually count the column number again every day

options.label: C
options.text: Convert all numbers to text randomly

questions.explanation: XLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH are more robust than hard-coded VLOOKUP column indexes.

questions.title: Excel
questions.prompt: A pivot table shows inflated revenue because the source has multiple rows per order after joining orders to items. What should the analyst check first?

options.label: A
options.text: Grain of the dataset and whether order-level revenue is duplicated after the join

options.label: B
options.text: Font size of the pivot table

options.label: C
options.text: Whether the worksheet is zoomed in

questions.explanation: Inflation after joins usually indicates grain mismatch or duplicated measures.
